issues.title: [Epic · Week 1] Product, data, legal, and UI discovery gate
issues.outcome: Complete the product, data, legal, and UI discovery gate without pulling later platform work into the current gate.
scope: Own the phase dependency order.
Surface risks and scope cuts.
Close the phase only with evidence.
acceptanceCriteria: Every child issue has a clear owner, milestone, and acceptance criteria.
Blocked work is represented using issue dependencies.
The phase gate has explicit evidence.
definitionOfDone: Acceptance criteria are verified.
Relevant documentation and tests are updated.
No unreviewed legal, accessibility, security, or performance exception is introduced.

issues.title: [Week 1 · Product] Project charter, community promise, and production workflow
issues.outcome: Define why the project exists, who it serves, how community contributions work, and what production quality means for a static wiki MVP.
scope: Write the charter and contribution principles.
Define branch, commit, PR, review, and evidence expectations.
Define Definition of Ready and Definition of Done.
State the unofficial fan-project position.
acceptanceCriteria: Charter distinguishes passion project, community product, and portfolio evidence.
Workflow is practical for one maintainer using AI collaborators.
Production quality is defined without unnecessary infrastructure.
definitionOfDone: Acceptance criteria are verified.
Relevant documentation and tests are updated.
No unreviewed legal, accessibility, security, or performance exception is introduced.

issues.title: [Week 1 · Product] Wiki-first PRD, MVP boundaries, sitemap, and user journeys
issues.outcome: Replace the viewer-first MVP definition with a wiki-first product definition for ordinary Arcaea players.
scope: Define personas and primary tasks.
Approve routes and content taxonomy.
Define homepage, explore, song, pack, partner, story, game-topic, information, settings, legal, and error surfaces.
Define explicit cuts and post-MVP path.
acceptanceCriteria: PRD matches docs/product/WEB_MVP_BRIEF.md.
URL query parameters own sort/filter state.
Runtime, upload, playback, analytics, and replay are excluded from the MVP navigation.
Primary user journeys are testable.
definitionOfDone: Acceptance criteria are verified.
Relevant documentation and tests are updated.
No unreviewed legal, accessibility, security, or performance exception is introduced.

issues.title: [Week 1 · UX] Research references and approve an original Arcaea-inspired UI direction
issues.outcome: Produce an original desktop-first UI direction based on broad reference research rather than copying Sekai Viewer or a wiki.
scope: Research at least eight relevant sites.
Document information architecture, navigation, cards/tables, filters, image treatment, and accessibility findings.
Create wireframes and a high-fidelity prototype for home, explore, and song detail.
Test Hikari-inspired light and Tairitsu-inspired dark token candidates.
acceptanceCriteria: Accepted UI direction includes rejected alternatives and reasons.
Both themes pass preliminary contrast review.
Component inventory and state matrix exist.
Design notes include responsive, performance, and reduced-motion implications.
definitionOfDone: Acceptance criteria are verified.
Relevant documentation and tests are updated.
No unreviewed legal, accessibility, security, or performance exception is introduced.

issues.title: [Week 1 · Data] Approve static versioned catalog architecture and content access contract
issues.outcome: Choose a static, versioned JSON/data-module architecture that supports wiki pages without a backend.
scope: Define catalog boundaries and IDs.
Define build-time validation and generated indexes.
Define browser loading, caching, stale-version, and failure behavior.
Record triggers that would justify a backend later.
acceptanceCriteria: ADR selects static versioned data for MVP.
No backend is added.
Contracts support songs, charts, packs, partners, story, game topics, sources, and assets.
Future migration path does not leak infrastructure into current code.
definitionOfDone: Acceptance criteria are verified.
Relevant documentation and tests are updated.
No unreviewed legal, accessibility, security, or performance exception is introduced.

issues.title: [Week 1 · Legal] Fan-project, provenance, source, and licensed-asset guardrails
issues.outcome: Define what facts, prose, links, and media may be published and how permission evidence is retained.
scope: Adopt the official-first source hierarchy.
Define facts-only use of unlicensed sources.
Require source and asset records.
Define takedown and correction contact path.
Define forbidden repository content.
acceptanceCriteria: Every content entity can reference source records.
Every published image has permission evidence or an accepted open license.
Community wiki prose and assets are not copied without compatible permission.
Public disclaimer and takedown process are specified.
definitionOfDone: Acceptance criteria are verified.
Relevant documentation and tests are updated.
No unreviewed legal, accessibility, security, or performance exception is introduced.

issues.title: [Week 1 · Planning] Canonical six-week execution order, dependencies, and scope cuts
issues.outcome: Make the issue hierarchy and six-week gate order the single execution source of truth.
scope: Map all existing issues to current MVP, deferred, superseded, or post-MVP.
Document capacity and weekly gates.
Add dependency relationships and sub-issues.
Document what to cut first if schedule slips.
acceptanceCriteria: docs/roadmap/WEB_MVP_ROADMAP.md is canonical.
No active MVP issue depends on runtime features.
Every issue has phase, priority, area, milestone, and ownership labels.
Superseded issues explain their replacement.
definitionOfDone: Acceptance criteria are verified.
Relevant documentation and tests are updated.
No unreviewed legal, accessibility, security, or performance exception is introduced.

issues.title: [Week 1 · Data] Define metadata schemas, provenance model, and legal seed catalog
issues.outcome: Define the normalized records and create a small internally consistent seed catalog for product development.
scope: Schemas for songs, charts, packs, partners, story entries, game topics, sources, assets, and releases.
Stable IDs and cross-entity references.
Small seed catalog with permitted placeholders/assets.
Validation rules and content-size limits.
acceptanceCriteria: Schemas reject missing IDs, broken references, invalid URLs, duplicate keys, and unreviewed assets.
Seed data exercises every MVP page template.
Each record has game-version and review metadata.
No protected chart file or audio is required.
definitionOfDone: Acceptance criteria are verified.
Relevant documentation and tests are updated.
No unreviewed legal, accessibility, security, or performance exception is introduced.
